context.env: Real Postgres: DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations 0001-0006 applied). turbo test task is cache:false.
invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code.
workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or the trusted message body set by an earlier server step.

LOCAL-only: SES (SendEmail), SNS, and the onboarding DNS/SES-status checks are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock + injected resolvers). NEVER send real mail or hit real DNS/SES. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433.

Sending is gated on verification: the Dispatcher refuses to send for any workspace whose status is not active/verified (§10). Every send passes suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours, in that order.

Suppression is per-workspace: keyed (workspace_id, email); an unsubscribe/bounce in workspace A must NOT suppress the same email in workspace B. Hard bounces are ALSO recorded in the cross-workspace global_hard_bounces.
Idempotency: outbox.dedupe_key prevents double-sends; feedback handlers are idempotent on the SES message id / event.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin and are unit-tested via the pure function. Reuse the Phase 2-5 harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations, runPlanInWorkspaceTx) and the SqlStatement {text, values} shape.
Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id is a GLOBAL PK). No cross-file races.

Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no broadcasts (§9A), automations (§9B), image pipeline, frontend, or metering (those are later phases). Leave clean extension points.

criteria: A hard bounce suppresses the email in-workspace (and is recorded in global_hard_bounces); a complaint suppresses in-workspace and sets email_status=complained.
Suppression scoping: an unsubscribe in workspace A does NOT suppress the same email in workspace B.
Reputation policing: a workspace exceeding bounce/complaint thresholds is auto-suspended (status=suspended) WITHOUT pausing other workspaces.
The feedback handler is idempotent (re-delivered SES notifications do not double-write suppressions/email_events); soft bounces suppress only after N.
scope: services/feedback: SNS-triggered Feedback Lambda (resolve workspace from the message/identity). Hard bounce -> suppressions (workspace_id, email, hard_bounce) + profiles.email_status=bounced + add to global_hard_bounces. Complaint -> suppressions (complaint) + email_status=complained. Soft bounce -> count; suppress after N. Record every event in email_events (workspace-scoped). Idempotent on the SES message id.

Per-workspace reputation policing: compute per-workspace bounce/complaint rates from email_events and AUTO-SUSPEND a single workspace (workspaces.status=suspended) on threshold breach, WITHOUT affecting other workspaces.
services/unsubscribe: one-click Unsubscribe Lambda writes suppressions (workspace_id, email, unsubscribe) — workspace-scoped, so unsubscribing from A does not affect B. Honor List-Unsubscribe-Post.
Pure functions for bounce/complaint classification, soft-bounce-after-N, and the reputation threshold decision.
phase.specSections: §10 (feedback pipeline SNS->Feedback Lambda, suppression on bounce/complaint, reputation alarms + auto-suspend, one-click unsubscribe), §6 (suppressions, global_hard_bounces, email_events, messages_log, usage_counters)
phase.title: Feedback + compliance + reputation policing

implUnits: 1. migration 0007: UNIQUE index email_events(workspace_id,ses_message_id,type) for idempotency; confirm suppressions PK (workspace_id,email) + global_hard_bounces PK (email) exist

2. services/feedback/src/core.ts pure (classifySesEvent,extractRecipients,resolveWorkspaceRef,shouldSuppressSoftBounce,decideReputation+constants, all SqlStatement builders, throw on falsy workspaceId)

3. services/feedback/src/feedback.ts orchestrator buildFeedbackPlan + handleNotification (resolve workspace tag->config_set->identity via reader, resolve profile by (workspace,email), commit plan via runInWorkspaceTx, then reputation policing for that workspace)
4. services/feedback/src/handler.ts thin SNS handler (parse Records[].Sns.Message, per-record try/catch->batch failure, never throw)
5. services/feedback/src/deps.ts makeProdDeps (pooled reader + runStatementsInWorkspaceTx + now); replace placeholder
6. services/unsubscribe/src/core.ts parseUnsubscribeRequest + buildUnsubscribeSuppression (reason=unsubscribe ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), honor List-Unsubscribe-Post
7. services/unsubscribe/src/handler.ts+deps.ts thin handler scoping by workspace_id from link; replace placeholder
mocks: Synthetic SNS notification payloads (Bounce Permanent/Transient, Complaint, Delivery/Open/Click) as Records[].Sns.Message JSON strings
Injected reader + runInWorkspaceTx + now() clock deps; integration injects adminPool-backed reader + runStatementsInWorkspaceTx
NOT mocked: Postgres in integration tier (real localhost:5433)
plan.notes: Order: migration first (idempotency meaningful), then pure core (unblocks unit red->green), orchestrator, handlers, deps. Workspace resolution: NO workspace_id in SES payload -> resolve sender-side ONLY in priority: (a) mail.tags.workspace_id set at send time, (b) per-workspace Configuration Set name (sending_identity->>config_set) DB lookup, (c) sending identity/from-domain (sending_identity->>from_domain). Unresolved -> batch failure, never guessed/default (invariant 2). Idempotency key = mail.messageId in email_events.ses_message_id; UNIQUE (workspace_id,ses_message_id,type) + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING -> re-delivered SNS no-op; suppressions PK (workspace_id,email), global_hard_bounces PK (email) natural idempotency. Suppression scoping: per-workspace isolated A-vs-B; global_hard_bounces documented cross-workspace exception (hard bounce blocks everywhere via buildIsSuppressedQuery global arm); AC2 asserts both. Auto-suspend: rates per-workspace from email_events numerator + messages_log denominator + MIN_SENT_FOR_RATE guard; suspend ONLY offender; healthy unchanged. Reuse SqlStatement, runStatementsInWorkspaceTx/runPlanInWorkspaceTx, adminPool/hasDatabaseUrl skipIf, buildIsSuppressedQuery from dispatcher for Phase-7 cross-check. Service-role paths scope by workspace_id in code, tests on adminPool. Mock SES/SNS at synthetic-payload boundary only; never mock Postgres. Thresholds named constants: bounce ~5% critical, complaint ~0.1%; soft N default 3 asserted.

assertions: reads both notificationType AND Configuration-Set eventType shapes
extracts sesMessageId from mail.messageId + resolver fields
malformed -> unknown -> handler failure not silent drop
MIN_SENT_FOR_RATE guard (don't suspend on tiny sample)
falls back configurationSetName -> by:config_set DB lookup
none when no field -> handler fails not guess
NEVER trusts recipient/client-supplied id; sender-side only
buildEmailEventInsert binds workspace_id $1, ON CONFLICT (workspace_id,ses_message_id,type) DO NOTHING
buildSuppressionUpsert binds $1 ON CONFLICT (workspace_id,email) DO NOTHING
buildGlobalHardBounceUpsert ON CONFLICT (email) DO NOTHING cross-workspace
rate/count queries bind workspace_id $1 read email_events+messages_log
buildWorkspaceSuspend UPDATE workspaces status=suspended WHERE id=$1
hard bounce plan: email_events + suppression hard_bounce + profile bounced + global_hard_bounces
complaint plan: email_events + suppression complaint + profile complained, NO global
soft below N: email_events only NO suppression
unresolved workspace -> empty plan + unresolved signal
same ses_message_id twice -> no net new writes (ON CONFLICT)
unresolved/malformed -> batch item failure not silent drop, others processed
reputation policing invoked per affected workspace not globally
hard bounce -> one suppressions hard_bounce + profiles bounced + global_hard_bounces row + email_events
complaint -> suppression complaint + profiles complained, NO global, email_events
all rows resolved workspace_id, nothing under other workspace
A and B both have profile for shared email
hard bounce: global_hard_bounces makes query true in BOTH, but per-workspace suppressions only in A
OFFENDER + HEALTHY workspaces with messages_log denominator + email_events
reputation policing reads rate from email_events+messages_log per workspace
same hard-bounce ses_message_id twice -> one suppressions, one global, one email_events row
different ses_message_id same email -> second email_events but still one suppressions (PK workspace_id,email)
N-1 distinct soft bounces -> N-1 email_events, NO suppression
count per-workspace (other workspace's soft bounce doesn't advance)
replay duplicate ses_message_id doesn't advance count
suppression stmt binds workspace_id $1 reason=unsubscribe source one-click
GET/non-one-click per policy, never writes other workspace
later B unsubscribe -> independent second row
replay idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING single A row
cross-check Phase-7 buildIsSuppressedQuery A true B false

instructions: Read CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md sections: §10 (feedback pipeline SNS->Feedback Lambda, suppression on bounce/complaint, reputation alarms + auto-suspend, one-click unsubscribe), §6 (suppressions, global_hard_bounces, email_events, messages_log, usage_counters).
FIRST write the failing tests from the plan (red), then implement to green. Logic lives in pure injected functions; handlers only wire them up.
Honor every invariant in context.invariants — tenant isolation, suppression scoping, the verification gate, and idempotency are pass/fail.

MOCK SES (SendEmail/identity/config-set), SNS, and DNS/SES-status checks (aws-sdk-client-mock + injected resolvers). NEVER send real mail or hit real DNS/SES. Integration tests run against the REAL local Postgres; do NOT mock the DB. Reuse the Phase 2-5 testutil harness.

Run the relevant `pnpm test` (with DATABASE_URL set), `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`. Iterate until GREEN, including a COLD turbo run. Confirm no regression in phases 2-5. Report the actual command output summary.
Implement ONLY what the cited sections require — no broadcasts/automations/image/frontend/metering.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Feedback + compliance + reputation policing" to satisfy its test plan and §18 criteria. Red -> green -> refactor.
